conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first read through the official eligibility criteria, and then I'll ask you some questions about your circumstances.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. I've reviewed the eligibility criteria. To help determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions.

First, do you have any children? If yes, how many children are you inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! Yes, I have one child - Alex. I'd like to know if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

First, can you tell me how old Alex is?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Since Alex is 17, I need to check if he's in approved education or training.

Is Alex currently in approved education or training (such as full-time non-advanced education, or approved training like an unpaid work-based programme)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in education or training at the moment. He left school recently, but he has registered with the careers service to figure out what he wants to do next.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. That's helpful to know that Alex has registered with the careers service.

Can you tell me approximately when Alex left education? I need to understand if it's been within the last 20 weeks or not.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: It's been quite recent - just a couple of months ago, actually.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you, that's helpful. Now I need to understand your living situation with Alex.

Do you currently live with Alex in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester - and Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or has anyone else claimed it recently?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. One final question: Are you or your partner (if you have one) earning over £60,000 per year individually? This won't affect your eligibility, but it's important to know as there may be a tax charge that applies.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of us earns over £60,000 a year.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:

Although Alex is 17 and has left education, the rules allow Child Benefit to continue for up to **20 weeks** after a 16 or 17 year old leaves education or training, provided they register with either a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services. Since Alex registered with the careers service just a couple of months ago, you're still within this 20-week extension period.

You also meet the other key requirements:
- You live with Alex in the UK
- You're responsible for him
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him
- Neither you nor your partner earns over £60,000, so you won't have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge

**Important note:** The 20-week extension period is temporary, so if Alex hasn't started approved education, training, or work by the time the 20 weeks is up, your Child Benefit entitlement for Alex would end. It would be a good idea to keep track of when the 20 weeks expires from when he left education.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in the 20-week extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Child is not in local authority care

Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Since Alex has registered with the careers service and it has only been a couple of months (well within the 20-week extension period), you remain eligible for Child Benefit. Additionally, you live with Alex in the UK, you are responsible for him, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him, which satisfies the basic eligibility requirements.
